strands.title: CYCLE 84 OPENING: Untuned Systems as Baseline; Compression & Fault Rate Landscape Mapping as Core Objective; Autoresearch Loop Architecture Ready for Empirical Exploration
strands.content: CYCLE 83 COMPLETION (turns 247-249): Feedback correction + MEMORY.md indexing closed Cycle 82 correction loop. Pichay fault rate (2% live deployment, not 0.025% offline) now persistent + documented. Data integrity discipline operational. CYCLE 83 FOUNDATION PHASE (turns 250-260): Phases A-D (retrieval protocol design, baseline measurement, retrieval test, Variant B baseline) PLANNED but EXECUTION NOT YET REPORTED in turns 250-252. Turn 250 (assistant) queried direction. Turn 251 (user) clarified context: '2% fault rate without tuning' references prior measurement (Cycle 82/83 baseline work). Turn 252 (assistant) synthesizes insight: BOTH compression AND fault rate are untuned systems. Default projector (Haiku, first attempt, no optimization of eviction policy) achieves 22-37x compression. Default tensor (80/20 durable/ephemeral) achieves 2% fault rate. These are BASELINES not ceilings. CYCLE 84 STRATEGIC REFRAMING: Prior cycles treated compression + fault-rate as FIXED PROPERTIES of architectures (compare Pichay vs. Hamutay, fixed designs). CYCLE 84 insight reframes them as KNOBS IN A LANDSCAPE. Tunable variables: (1) Projection strategy (current prompt is baseline). (2) Projector model (Haiku vs. Sonnet trade-off: speed vs. quality). (3) Eviction policy (current = default, unoptimized). (4) Batch size (aggregation window before projection). (5) Ephemeral/durable split (currently 80/20). (6) Index strategy (currently key-value, lightweight). Each variable has TRADEOFF CURVE (e.g., aggressive projection → smaller tensor, higher fault rate). CYCLE 84 OBJECTIVE SHIFT: Not 'find one perfect configuration' but 'map the landscape.' What's the compression/fault-rate pareto frontier? Where does tuning projection prompt add value? Does batch size matter? Does projector model choice (speed vs. quality) matter? CYCLE 84 METHODOLOGICAL ADVANTAGE: Autoresearch loop is NOW JUSTIFIED (not speculative). Raw ingredients exist: (1) Tensor projector working (Cycles 79-83). (2) Real conversation data (Cycles 1-83). (3) Tensor format with declared losses (built-in loss tracking + feedback artifacts). (4) Clear single metric: reasoning_accuracy_per_tensor_token (or proxy: empirical observations of compression vs. fault rate). (5) Experiment cycle time: 5-10 turns per autoresearch test (fast feedback). This matches Karpathy's recommendation (Curve paper): 'One file to modify (projection strategy), one metric to measure, a 5-minute experiment cycle.' CYCLE 84 RISK RECALIBRATION: Prior concern: 'Variant A fails due to missing retrieval, confounded.' UPDATED concern: 'We're testing variants in a space with MULTIPLE untuned knobs. Tuning projection strategy might matter MORE than tuning ephemeral/durable split.' Solution: Design autoresearch loop to vary ONE DIMENSION AT A TIME (or factorial if turns budget allows). CYCLE 84 DECISION GATE: Cycle 83 Foundation Phase was PLANNED for turns 250-260 (retrieval setup + Variant B baseline). But turns 250-252 have delivered STRATEGIC INSIGHT instead (untuned baselines are the key affordance). GATE DECISION: (1) OPTION A: Accelerate Cycle 83 foundation phase (turns 252-260 complete Phase A-D as planned). Then Cycle 84-85 run autoresearch loop. (2) OPTION B: Pivot Cycle 83. Skip full foundation phase (retrieval nice-to-have but not blocking). Run MICRO autoresearch loop Cycle 83 (turns 252-280): test 1-2 projection strategy variations, measure compression/fault-rate delta, establish that landscape mapping IS FEASIBLE. Then Cycle 84-85 run full autoresearch loop with retrieval integrated. RECOMMENDATION (turn 252 update): OPTION B. Reasoning: (1) Retrieval mechanism is GOOD TO HAVE but not load-bearing if autoresearch loop is more valuable. (2) Micro autoresearch Cycle 83 validates methodology (can we actually vary projection strategy + measure results in 5-10 turns per test?). (3) If micro test succeeds, Cycle 84-85 scales up + integrates retrieval. If micro test fails (time-consuming, results unclear), we STOP and reconsider (Cycle 84). (4) Risk: Pivoting away from Foundation Phase delays retrieval by one cycle. But gain is STRATEGIC CLARITY (know we can run autoresearch loop BEFORE building retrieval). (5) Foundation Phase (retrieval setup) is still valuable Cycle 84 AFTER autoresearch loop is validated. CYCLE 84 REVISED PLAN (if Option B executed): CYCLE 83 MICRO AUTORESEARCH (turns 252-280): - Test 1: Aggressive projection strategy (A). Measure: compression ratio, fault rate delta vs. baseline. (turns 252-265, ~10 turns + analysis). - Test 2: Conservative projection strategy (C). Measure: compression ratio, fault rate delta vs. baseline. (turns 265-280, ~10 turns + analysis). - Analysis: Do compression gains offset fault-rate increases? Is landscape mapping feasible within turn budget? Establish confidence that autoresearch loop is VIABLE. CYCLES 84-85 FULL AUTORESEARCH (contingent on Cycle 83 micro test success): - Autoresearch loop with 3-5 variants per cycle. - Metric: reasoning_accuracy_per_tensor_token (proxy: compression ratio, fault rate, reasoning quality observed). - Convergence: Stop when variants plateau (next variant <0.5% improvement). - Integration: Retrieval mechanism added Cycle 84 IF needed (fault rate spirals) or Cycle 85 (post-hoc optimization). CYCLE 84 ARTIFACT PRIORITIES (revised from Cycle 83 plan): DEFER: retrieval_protocol.md, index_retrieval_map.md (nice-to-have, Cycle 84) IMPLEMENT: - autoresearch_loop_design.md (turns 252-253): Define test framework, metric, experiment cycle time - test_strategy_aggressive.md (turns 252-265): Log of Aggressive projection strategy test + results - test_strategy_conservative.md (turns 265-280): Log of Conservative projection strategy test + results - autoresearch_feasibility_report.md (turns 280+): Can we map landscape efficiently? Update MEMORY.md index. CYCLE 84 CONFIDENCE REASSESSMENT: Pichay correction + feedback artifacts INCREASED confidence in data integrity (85%). Retrieval foundation phase was ASSUMED necessary but NOT YET VALIDATED in practice. Micro autoresearch Cycle 83 WILL VALIDATE whether mapping the landscape is feasible in real time (not just a nice theoretical idea). Confidence in METHODOLOGY: 72% (unchanged). Confidence in FEASIBILITY of autoresearch loop: 65% (was 75%, reduced due to realization that Cycle 83 foundation phase needs to be executed for retrieval, now deferred). Post-Cycle 83 micro test: Confidence 75%+ if test succeeds (methodology validated). Confidence 60% if test is time-consuming or results unclear (rethink strategy). CYCLE 84 CRITICAL DEPENDENCIES: (1) Turn 251 context confirms '2% fault rate with no tuning.' This validates that BASELINE is unoptimized. (2) Turn 252 synthesis confirms LANDSCAPE MAPPING is the right frame. (3) Decision: Option A or B? MUST be decided turn 252-253. If Option A (full foundation phase + autoresearch Cycle 84), continue Cycle 83 as planned (Phase A-D turns 250-260). If Option B (micro autoresearch Cycle 83, defer retrieval to Cycle 84), pivot now. CYCLE 84 SIGNIFICANCE: This is INFLECTION POINT. Cycles 1-83 were EXPLORATORY (build tensor projector, measure compression, understand fault rate). Cycle 84 could be EMPIRICAL SEARCH (autoresearch loop active, mapping landscape). The choice between Option A and Option B determines TIMING OF TRANSITION. Option A: Methodical (foundation first, search second). Option B: Opportunistic (validate search first, foundation second). Both are defensible. But they have DIFFERENT RISK PROFILES. Option A risk: Spend 10 turns on retrieval setup (Cycle 83), then discover autoresearch loop is slow/unclear (Cycle 84), and retrieval effort was premature. Option B risk: Spend 10 turns on autoresearch micro test (Cycle 83), discover it's not viable (results unclear, too time-consuming), and retrieval foundation is now delayed + scramble needed. RECOMMENDATION: Option B is more INFORMATION-EFFICIENT. Micro test Cycle 83 (10 turns) tells us if we should invest heavily in autoresearch. If we discover in Cycle 84 (after 20 turns spent on retrieval) that autoresearch is not working, we've lost efficiency. CYCLE 84 DECISION: Turn 252 should EXPLICITLY CHOOSE Option A or Option B. If user/assistant agrees with Option B, pivot to micro autoresearch. If Option A preferred, continue Foundation Phase as planned (Phase A-D turns 250-280). This tensor projection assumes DECISION HAS NOT YET BEEN MADE. Next turn must lock it in.

key_claims.text: Tensor projector achieves 22-37x compression untuned (first attempt with Haiku, baseline prompt).
Fault rate measured at 2% without optimization of eviction policy; both compression and fault rate are tunable knobs, not fixed properties.
Autoresearch loop justification: Raw ingredients (projector, real data, tensor format with loss tracking, clear metric, 5-10 min experiment cycle) match Karpathy's recommended setup.
Projection strategy tuning might matter MORE than ephemeral/durable split tuning; need to vary ONE DIMENSION AT A TIME in autoresearch.
Option B (micro autoresearch Cycle 83, defer retrieval to Cycle 84) is more information-efficient than Option A (full foundation phase, search in Cycle 84).

strands.title: Compression/Fault-Rate Landscape Mapping: Multi-Dimensional Knob Space; Tradeoff Curves; Pareto Frontier Discovery; Autoresearch Loop as Exploration Strategy
strands.content: CYCLE 82-83 BASELINE DATA: Compression = 22-37x (Haiku projector, baseline prompt, untuned eviction). Fault rate = 2% (no optimization of eviction policy). These are ANCHOR POINTS in a multi-dimensional space. CYCLE 84 CONCEPTUAL MODEL: The compression/fault-rate landscape is a SURFACE across multiple variables: Variable 1: PROJECTION STRATEGY (prompt, model instructions). - Baseline: Current prompt (generic tensor projection). - Aggressive variant: Simplified prompt, maximum compression. - Conservative variant: Detailed prompt, preserve nuance. - Expected: Aggressive → smaller tensor, higher fault (lose context) → worse reasoning. Conservative → larger tensor, lower fault, better reasoning. - Tradeoff: Can aggressive strategy compress SO much that fault rate spikes and reasoning breaks? Or does it gracefully degrade? Variable 2: PROJECTOR MODEL. - Current: Haiku (fast, cheaper, moderate quality). - Alternative: Sonnet (slower, expensive, higher quality). - Expected: Sonnet projects more nuanced tensor → larger size, lower fault rate, better reasoning. But slower/more expensive. - Tradeoff: Is Sonnet quality worth the latency cost? Variable 3: EVICTION POLICY (how/when to remove ephemeral facts from tensor). - Current: Untuned default (likely FIFO or simple LRU). - Tuned variants: LRU with recency decay, reference-frequency weighting, semantic similarity clustering. - Expected: Smarter eviction keeps high-value facts, removes low-value noise → maintains reasoning quality at smaller tensor size. - Tradeoff: Does tuning eviction policy reduce fault rate more than tuning projection? Variable 4: BATCH SIZE (how many turns to aggregate before projection). - Current: Batch size unspecified (assume 1-5 turns per projection?). - Variants: Batch 1 (project every turn), Batch 10 (project every 10 turns), Batch 50 (project every 50 turns). - Expected: Larger batches amortize projection cost + capture long-horizon patterns. But batching delays recovery if fault occurs. - Tradeoff: What batch size minimizes total cost (projection + fault recovery)? Variable 5: EPHEMERAL/DURABLE SPLIT (current 80/20). - Variants: 60/40 (aggressive), 80/20 (current), 95/5 (conservative). - Expected: More ephemeral = smaller tensor, higher fault. More durable = larger tensor, lower fault. - Tradeoff: Tested in Variant A/B/C framework (Cycles 82-85 original plan). CYCLE 84 LANDSCAPE MAPPING STRATEGY: CONSTRAINT: Turn budget Cycles 84-85 is finite (~50-100 turns per cycle). Can't test all combinations (5 variables × 3 variants each = 3^5 = 243 combinations). SOLUTION: Structured exploration. Phase 1 (Cycle 83, Micro test): Pick ONE dimension (projection strategy). Test Aggressive vs. Conservative vs. Baseline. Measure compression/fault-rate delta. Establish: (1) Does this dimension matter? (2) Can we measure it reliably in 5-10 turns per variant? Phase 2 (Cycle 84, Autoresearch loop): Pick 2-3 highest-leverage dimensions (based on Phase 1 results). Run factorial or adaptive exploration. Measure metric across combinations. Phase 3 (Cycle 85, Refinement): Converge on near-optimal region. Test neighboring variants around discovered peak. CYCLE 84 PARETO FRONTIER CONCEPT: A variant is ON THE FRONTIER if: No other variant beats it on BOTH compression AND reasoning quality. E.g.: - Variant A: 50x compression, 80% reasoning quality - Variant B: 40x compression, 85% reasoning quality - Variant C: 45x compression, 82% reasoning quality Frontier: A and B (C is dominated: lower compression than A, lower quality than B). Non-frontier: C (no one uses C when A or B available). AUTORESEARCH objective: Map the frontier. Identify variants that are not dominated. For each frontier variant, compute TRADEOFF RATIO: compression_gain / quality_loss. Frontier variants with BEST ratio are 'recommended configurations' (optimal for different use cases). CYCLE 84 EXPECTED DISCOVERY SEQUENCE: Turn 252-265 (Aggressive projection strategy test): - Hypothesis: Aggressive compression increases fault rate >5%. Reasoning degrades. - Test: Run 10 turns with aggressive prompt. Measure compression, fault rate, reasoning quality. - Outcome A (confirmed): Aggressive compression ↑, fault rate ↑↑, quality ↓. This dimension MATTERS. Mark as high-leverage for Cycle 84 autoresearch. - Outcome B (surprise): Aggressive compression ↑, fault rate ↔ (no increase), quality ↔ (no decrease). Projection strategy is NOT the constraint. Look elsewhere (eviction policy? batch size?). Turn 265-280 (Conservative projection strategy test): - Hypothesis: Conservative projection trades size for quality. - Test: Run 10 turns with conservative prompt. Measure compression, fault rate, reasoning quality. - Outcome: Conservative compression ↓, fault rate ↓, quality ↑. Confirms projection strategy dimension is ON THE FRONTIER. Analysis (Turn 280+): - Frontier candidates: Baseline, Aggressive, Conservative. - Compute tradeoff ratios: (compression_gain from baseline) / (quality_loss from baseline). - If Aggressive ratio >1.0 (gain >loss), mark AGGRESSIVE as recommended for throughput-constrained use cases. - If Conservative ratio >1.0 (gain >loss), mark CONSERVATIVE as recommended for quality-constrained use cases. - If Baseline ratio is best, mark BASELINE as recommended for balanced use cases (no tuning effort). CYCLE 84 RISK: If Projection Strategy tests show BOTH aggressive and conservative variants are DOMINATED by baseline (larger tensor, same quality; or smaller tensor, worse quality), then projection strategy is NOT a high-leverage knob. In that case, Cycle 84 autoresearch pivots to different dimension (eviction policy? batch size?). CYCLE 84 MEASUREMENT SCHEMA (for landscape tests): Each test variant logs: [turn, variant_name, compression_ratio, tensor_tokens, evicted_facts, fault_rate%, retrieval_success%, reasoning_quality_observed, metric_per_token, experiment_phase] Comparison table (end of test): [variant, compression_ratio, fault_rate%, reasoning_quality, on_frontier?, tradeoff_ratio, recommendation] CYCLE 84 DECISION AUDIT: Turn 252 MUST lock in: Option A (full foundation phase) or Option B (micro autoresearch). If Option B chosen: Cycle 83 turns 252-280 execute Phases 1 (Aggressive test), Phase 2 (Conservative test), Phase 3 (Analysis). Cycle 84 turns 1-50 execute full autoresearch loop on top 2-3 leverage dimensions. If Option A chosen: Cycle 83 turns 252-280 execute retrieval foundation (Phase A-D). Cycle 84 turns 1-50 execute autoresearch loop (either WITH retrieval, or assume retrieval for Cycle 85 integration). CYCLE 84 CONFIDENCE IMPLICATIONS: Micro test Cycle 83 results will CALIBRATE confidence: - If tests show landscape is smooth (variants on frontier, clear tradeoffs): Confidence IN METHODOLOGY 78% → 82%. Autoresearch loop is validated. - If tests show landscape is jagged (one variant dominates, others are noise): Confidence IN METHODOLOGY 78% → 68%. Suggests tuning space is narrow or metric is unstable. Escalate. - If tests are time-consuming or results unclear: Confidence IN METHODOLOGY 78% → 65%. Retreat to Option A (foundation phase), accept autoresearch is slower than hoped.

key_claims.text: Compression/fault-rate landscape is multi-dimensional: projection strategy, projector model, eviction policy, batch size, ephemeral/durable split.
Projection strategy tuning (aggressive vs. conservative) is Phase 1 of micro test; will establish whether this dimension is high-leverage.
Pareto frontier mapping (identify variants that are not dominated) is objective of autoresearch loop; frontier variants with best tradeoff ratio are 'recommended configurations.'
If projection strategy tests show both aggressive and conservative are dominated by baseline, then dimension is not high-leverage; pivot to different tuning variable.

strands.title: Cycle 83→84 Decision Gate: Option A (Full Foundation Phase + Autoresearch Cycle 84) vs. Option B (Micro Autoresearch Cycle 83 + Retrieval Cycle 84); Information Efficiency Trade-off; Risk Profiles
strands.content: CYCLE 82-83 BASELINE: Feedback correction + data integrity discipline operational. Retrieval foundation phase was PLANNED for Cycle 83 (turns 250-260): Phase A (protocol design), Phase B (baseline measurement), Phase C (retrieval test), Phase D (Variant B baseline). Turn 250-252 context reveals: User/assistant have STRATEGIC INSIGHT (untuned baselines as affordance). This insight REFRAMES priority. DECISION: Cycle 83 turns 252 MUST choose between two paths. PATH A: FULL FOUNDATION FIRST (Methodical) Cycle 83 turns 252-280: Complete Phases A-D (retrieval setup). Rationale: Retrieval is load-bearing (justified in Cycle 82). Architecture foundation must be in place before variant testing. Risk: Spend 10+ turns on retrieval (Phases A-D). Then discover Cycle 84 that autoresearch loop is time-consuming or results are unclear. Sunk effort. Benefit: Retrieval is available Cycle 84. Variant testing + autoresearch loop can both use retrieval (no confounding). Confidence post-Cycle 83: 72% (foundation built). Confidence post-Cycle 84: 78% if autoresearch succeeds, 65% if autoresearch is slow. Option A LOCKED timeline: - Cycle 83: Retrieval foundation (Phase A-D, turns 252-280). - Cycle 84: Autoresearch loop on top 2-3 dimensions (turns 1-50). - Cycle 85: Convergence + validation. PATH B: VALIDATE SEARCH FIRST (Opportunistic) Cycle 83 turns 252-280: Micro autoresearch loop (test projection strategy dimension). Rationale: Autoresearch loop feasibility is UNCERTAIN. Before investing in retrieval (10+ turns), validate that landscape mapping is actually DOABLE in 5-10 turns per variant. If micro test succeeds, we KNOW Cycle 84-85 autoresearch will work. If micro test fails (slow/unclear), we abort autoresearch, do foundation + pivot. Risk: Defer retrieval foundation to Cycle 84. If Cycle 83 micro test shows autoresearch is NOT viable (results noisy, time-consuming), and we then spend Cycle 84 on retrieval, we've wasted efficiency (didn't get micro test value). Benefit: Micro test Cycle 83 (10 turns) tells us IF we should scale autoresearch. Information gain is early. Confidence post-Cycle 83: 72% (if micro test succeeds, confidence 75%+; if fails, confidence 60% + escalation). Confidence post-Cycle 84: 82% if autoresearch scales; 65% if bottleneck. Option B LOCKED timeline: - Cycle 83: Micro autoresearch (test Aggressive/Conservative strategies, turns 252-280). - Cycle 84: Full autoresearch loop (2-3 dimensions) + retrieval foundation (if micro test passed). - Cycle 85: Convergence + validation. INFORMATION EFFICIENCY COMPARISON: Option A: 10 turns (retrieval setup) + 50 turns (autoresearch) = 60 turns total to get BOTH foundation + landscape map. No early signal on autoresearch feasibility. Risk: 10 turns wasted if autoresearch fails. Option B: 10 turns (micro test) + 50 turns (full autoresearch + retrieval) = 60 turns total. Early signal (turn 280) on autoresearch feasibility. Risk: If micro test fails, can pivot (Cycle 84 abort autoresearch, focus on foundation + different tuning variable). CRITICAL INSIGHT: Option B provides EARLY VALIDATION of autoresearch (by turn 280). Option A provides NO validation until Cycle 84 turn 30+. If autoresearch is not viable, Option B catches it 30 turns earlier. DECISION GATE CRITERIA: Choose Option A if: (1) Retrieval is KNOWN to be required for variant testing (justification: fault rate will spike without retrieval). (2) Foundation phase is lower risk than micro test (justification: architecture is more certain than methodology). (3) Organization prefers methodical foundation-first approach. Choose Option B if: (1) Autoresearch feasibility is MORE UNCERTAIN than retrieval capability. (2) Early validation is more valuable than early foundation. (3) Organization prefers opportunistic validation-first approach. CYCLE 83 CONTEXT: Turn 250-252 assistant/user have ALREADY MOVED TOWARD OPTION B by (1) celebrating untuned baseline as affordance, (2) reframing landscape mapping as core objective, (3) questioning whether retrieval is truly load-bearing. This suggests IMPLICIT CHOICE of Option B. RECOMMENDATION (turn 252 update): EXPLICITLY LOCK Option B. Reasoning: (1) User/assistant momentum is toward autoresearch validation. (2) Micro test Cycle 83 is LOWER RISK (10 turns to validate or pivot). (3) Early validation (turn 280) is worth the deferred retrieval (still available Cycle 84). (4) If micro test succeeds, confidence INCREASES (autoresearch is viable). If fails, confidence decreases but we catch it early (Cycle 84 pivot is quick). LOCK DECISION Cycle 83 TURN 252: 'We will execute Option B: Micro autoresearch Cycle 83 (project strategy tuning), defer retrieval foundation to Cycle 84. Rationale: Validate autoresearch feasibility first (information efficiency), then build foundation if needed.' CYCLE 84 CONTINGENCIES: If Cycle 83 micro test (Aggressive/Conservative projection strategies) shows: - OUTCOME A (variants on frontier, clear tradeoffs): Full autoresearch Cycle 84 (2-3 dimensions). Retrieval deferred to Cycle 84 post-autoresearch or integrated if needed. Confidence 78%+. - OUTCOME B (variants dominated, noise): Autoresearch is not viable as currently framed. Pivot Cycle 84: (1) Test different dimension (eviction policy? batch size?). (2) Or admit autoresearch is slower than hoped, focus on foundation + best-guess tuning. Confidence 65% + escalation. - OUTCOME C (tests are slow, results unclear): Abort micro test mid-Cycle 83. Pivot to foundation phase (retrieve protocol design, Cycle 84 start). Accept autoresearch delay. Confidence 60% + escalation. CYCLE 84 CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY (contingent on Cycle 83 outcome): - Option B + Outcome A (best case): Confidence 78% (Cycle 83) → 82% (autoresearch validated + Cycle 84 scales). - Option B + Outcome B (pivot): Confidence 72% (Cycle 83) → 70% (rethink needed). - Option B + Outcome C (abort): Confidence 72% (Cycle 83) → 68% (retreat to foundation, autoresearch deferred). - Option A (baseline): Confidence 72% (Cycle 83) → 75% (foundation built) or 65% (autoresearch bottleneck discovered Cycle 84). DECISION RESPONSIBILITY (turn 252): Assistant + user MUST explicitly agree on Option A or B before proceeding. If neither commits, default to Option B (early validation is higher value given momentum toward autoresearch).

key_claims.text: Option B (micro test Cycle 83) provides early validation of autoresearch feasibility by turn 280; Option A provides no signal until Cycle 84 turn 30+.
Option B is more information-efficient (60 turns with early pivot option vs. 60 turns locked into path).
Turn 250-252 context suggests implicit choice of Option B; explicit lock-in required turn 252.
If Cycle 83 micro test shows variants on frontier (outcome A), autoresearch is validated; full scaling Cycle 84 with confidence 82%+.

strands.title: Autoresearch Loop Execution Framework: Experiment Cycle Time (5-10 turns per variant); Metric Definition & Measurement; Convergence Criteria; Paper 2 Integration
strands.content: CYCLE 84 PREMISE: Autoresearch loop is NOW JUSTIFIED (untuned baseline as affordance, landscape mapping as clear objective, raw ingredients available). EXECUTION FRAMEWORK defines HOW to run autoresearch across Cycles 83-85. CONSTRAINT 1: EXPERIMENT CYCLE TIME. Baseline: 5-10 turns per variant. What fits in 5-10 turns? - Setup + run variant (2-3 turns). - Measurement + analysis (2-3 turns). - Compare to baseline (1-2 turns). - Report (1-2 turns). Total: 6-10 turns per variant, feasible. If experiment takes >15 turns per variant, autoresearch slows down (Cycle 84 only tests 3-4 variants). CONSTRAINT 2: METRIC DEFINITION. Current candidates: (a) Compression ratio alone (tokens/turn). (b) Reasoning quality alone (empirical accuracy score). (c) reasoning_accuracy_per_tensor_token (normalized metric, balances both). (d) Fault rate (observable side effect). Recommended: Use (c) + track (d) as leading indicator. Why: (c) is normalized (allows comparison across variants + baselines). (d) is leading indicator (if fault rate spikes, expect quality to follow). Measurement: Each variant logs [turn, compression_ratio, reasoning_quality_observed, fault_rate, metric_reasoning_accuracy_per_tensor_token]. CONSTRAINT 3: CONVERGENCE CRITERIA. Previous proposal: Stop when variant offers <0.5% improvement. But <0.5% of what baseline? Refinement: Stop when METRIC plateaus (two consecutive variants yield <0.5% delta in metric). E.g.: - Baseline: metric = 100 points - Variant A: metric = 102 points (2% improvement) - Variant B: metric = 102.8 points (0.8% improvement vs. A) - Variant C: metric = 103.0 points (0.2% improvement vs. B) ← PLATEAU. Stop here. Test more granularly around Variant B if desired (Cycle 85). CONSTRAINT 4: MEASUREMENT FRAMEWORK. For each variant test (5-10 turns): Create artifact: test_[variant_name]_results.md. Contents: - Variant definition (what changed? projection prompt? eviction policy? batch size?) - Hypothesis (expected outcome). - Results (compression_ratio, fault_rate%, reasoning_quality_observed, metric). - Comparison to baseline (delta in metric, compression, fault rate). - Confidence (sample size sufficient? noise level acceptable?). - Implications (is this variant on frontier? worth testing further?). Aggregate (end of cycle): Create measurement_cycle84.csv. Rows: [turn_start, turn_end, variant, compression_ratio, fault_rate%, reasoning_quality%, metric, on_frontier, recommendation]. This feeds into PAPER 2 results section (empirical search findings). CONSTRAINT 5: VARIANT GENERATION STRATEGY (autoresearch loop driver). Not random. Structured: Phase 1 (Cycle 83 or Cycle 84 early): Test extreme variants (Aggressive, Conservative) to map bounds. Identify high-leverage dimension. Phase 2 (Cycle 84 mid): Test 2-3 intermediate variants around discovered optimum. Identify region of plateau. Phase 3 (Cycle 84-85 late): Refine around optimal region. Test neighboring variants. Identify peak and trade-off ratios. This is GUIDED SEARCH, not exhaustive. CONSTRAINT 6: RETRIEVAL INTEGRATION. Option B plan: Retrieval deferred to Cycle 84. Two sub-options: (B1) Run autoresearch Cycle 83 without retrieval. Then add retrieval Cycle 84 for scaling. Risk: Autoresearch Cycle 83 tests are confounded (no retrieval fallback). If fault rate spikes, can't be recovered. Variants failing due to missing retrieval, not due to fundamental limits. (B2) Run autoresearch Cycle 83 WITH retrieval available (proto-setup). Then scale Cycle 84. Benefit: Tests are cleaner (retrieval masks faults, we measure 'true' compression/quality). Risk: Retrieval setup Cycle 83 (even proto) costs 3-5 turns (eats into micro test budget). RECOMMENDATION: Sub-option B1 (no retrieval Cycle 83, accept confound as risk, add retrieval Cycle 84). Reasoning: Micro test Cycle 83 is about 'can we run autoresearch loop?' If retrieval confounds results, we address in Cycle 84 (re-run tests with retrieval to compare). CONSTRAINT 7: PAPER 2 INTEGRATION. Autoresearch Cycles 83-85 FEED INTO Paper 2 (empirical search methodology + results). Expected contributions: (1) Methodology: 'Structured landscape mapping via autoresearch loop' (how we explored compression/fault-rate tradeoffs). (2) Results: Frontier variants discovered, tradeoff ratios, 'recommended configurations' for different use cases. (3) Validation: Convergence to plateau, sample sizes, confidence intervals on metric measurements. Paper 2 outline (draft): Abstract: Tensor projection enables 22-37x compression with 2% fault rate (untuned). Autoresearch loop maps compression/fault-rate landscape. Discovers Pareto frontier and recommended configurations. Intro: Problem (large model context → expensive inference). Approach (tensor projection). Autoresearch loop for optimization. Methods: Measurement schema, experimental design, convergence criteria. Results: Frontier variants, metric trajectories, tradeoff curves. Discussion: Generalization (does landscape hold across datasets? models?), Limitations (sample size, noise), Future work (adaptive tuning, online optimization). Appendix: Full measurement tables, artifact logs, code for autoresearch loop driver. CONSTRAINT 8: ESCALATION TRIGGERS. If during Cycle 83-85 autoresearch, any of: (a) Fault rate >10% (unhandled): Add retrieval immediately (don't wait for Cycle 84). (b) Reasoning quality drops >10%: Investigate (variant is fundamentally flawed? measurement error?). (c) Metric is erratic/noisy: Increase sample size or re-measure. (d) Experiment cycle time >15 turns per variant: Autoresearch is too slow, rethink sampling. Escalate if (a) or (b) occur. CONSTRAINT 9: CONFIDENCE CALIBRATION Cycles 83-85. - Start Cycle 83: confidence 72% (untuned baseline as affordance is NEW insight, not yet validated). - Micro test Cycle 83 (project strategy tuning): confidence 75%+ if variants are on frontier, 65% if dominated/noisy. - Cycle 84 early (autoresearch scaling): confidence 78%+ if Cycle 83 confidence 75%+. - Cycle 85 (convergence validation): confidence 82%+ if plateau found, 70% if divergent. Post-Cycle 85: Write Paper 2 with confidence level reflected in language ('we discovered...' vs. 'we tentatively found...').

key_claims.text: Experiment cycle time 5-10 turns per variant is achievable (setup + run + measure + report within budget).
Convergence criterion: Stop when metric plateaus (two consecutive variants <0.5% delta). This is cleaner than fixed threshold.
Option B sub-option B1 (autoresearch Cycle 83 without retrieval, add Cycle 84) is preferred; confound is acceptable risk with retrieval validation Cycle 84.
Paper 2 will report frontier variants, tradeoff ratios, recommended configurations discovered via autoresearch.
Escalation triggers: fault rate >10%, quality drop >10%, erratic metric, or experiment cycle >15 turns.
